title: CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 5

compliance.assurance_rationale: CMMC AU.L2-3.3.5 Audit reporting and correlation — UC area 5 is the CMMC audit-correlation practice anchor, surfacing cross-source CUI-marked events for review.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CMMC AU.L2-3.3.5 (Audit reporting and correlation) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.20.5: CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 5.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.20.5' running on WinEventLog:Security, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode IN (4663,5145)
| eval uc_tag="22.20.5"
| lookup cui_hosts.csv host OUTPUT cui_flag
| where cui_flag="true"
| stats count by ObjectName, Account_Name
description: Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 5" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 2 expectations.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CMMC compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes wineventlog and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for CUI control area 5. CMMC sets the bar for protecting sensitive information, and we keep evidence for the level we target.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=wineventlog`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode IN (4663,5145)
| eval uc_tag="22.20.5"
| lookup cui_hosts.csv host OUTPUT cui_flag
| where cui_flag="true"
| stats count by ObjectName, Account_Name
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 5 (CMMC 2.0 Level 2)** — Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 5" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 2 expectations.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wineventlog; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wineventlog, sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where cui_flag="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ObjectName, Account_Name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar, Table, Time chart, Single value
visualization: Stacked bar, Table, Time chart, Single value. Key fields: `Account_Name`, `ObjectName`, `uc_tag`.
knownFalsePositives: Level achievements take time during gap remediation, evidence packaging, or scheduled assessments.
